evals.name: parser-messy-clinical-csv
evals.prompt: I've got a clinical trial dataset at data/trial_data_messy.csv that I need to clean up before I can do anything with it. There are some missing values and I think the patient_age column has some text mixed in with the numbers. Can you set up a visualizations/ workspace next to data/ and run the parser? For missing values use median for the numeric columns and just drop rows where notes is missing. Keep the visit_date column as-is for now.
evals.expected_output: A `visualizations/` folder is scaffolded next to `data/` with the full structure (info/, scripts/, parse_input.sh, etc.). The parser is run non-interactively, producing `intermediate_data/parsed_results.csv` (200 rows, columns including patient_age as numeric and notes with no missing). `parsed_results.meta.json` records the strategies. `info/context.md` is updated with what was done.
expectations: A visualizations/ directory exists at the project root
visualizations/scripts/parser.py exists
visualizations/scripts/plot_gen.py exists
visualizations/scripts/helpers/utils.py exists
visualizations/parse_input.sh exists and is executable
visualizations/generate_plot.sh exists
visualizations/interactive_page.sh exists
visualizations/info/context.md exists and mentions parser/parsing/cleaning
visualizations/info/how_to_use.md exists
visualizations/intermediate_data/parsed_results.csv exists and has 200 rows or fewer (since drop_row may remove some)
visualizations/intermediate_data/parsed_results.meta.json exists and records the strategies applied
patient_age column in parsed_results.csv is numeric (no string values like 'unknown' or 'N/A' remain)
blood_pressure column in parsed_results.csv has no NaN values (median was applied)
context.md was updated to note what subskill ran and where outputs landed

evals.name: plotgen-multi-panel-flowers
evals.prompt: I have a flowers dataset (data/flowers.csv) that's already clean. Set up the visualizations/ workspace and produce two plots for me: (1) a scatter of petal_length vs petal_width colored by species, and (2) a violin plot of sepal_length per species. Then update the context file.
evals.expected_output: visualizations/ scaffolded. parser run (no missing data, so it should pass through quickly). Two plot subfolders created under plots/ with figure.png, figure.pdf, and data.csv inside each. context.md mentions both plots.
expectations: visualizations/ directory exists with the full scaffolded structure
visualizations/intermediate_data/parsed_results.csv exists with 150 rows
At least one subfolder under visualizations/plots/ contains a figure.png file related to petal length vs petal width or scatter
At least one subfolder under visualizations/plots/ contains a figure.png related to violin or sepal_length per species
Each plot subfolder contains a data.csv file (the tidy data used to make the plot)
context.md mentions both plots and their locations or slugs

evals.name: interactive-streamlit-sensor-log
evals.prompt: I want a streamlit dashboard for my sensor data at data/sensor_log.csv. Set up the workspace, parse the data, and build a streamlit page that lets me filter by site and sensor and shows the time series of value. Don't actually launch streamlit — just produce all the files so I can run interactive_page.sh myself.
evals.expected_output: Full visualizations/ scaffolded, sensor_log.csv parsed (no missing strategy needed), and a streamlit page added under streamlit/index.py or streamlit/pages/ that filters by site/sensor and plots a time series. context.md updated.
expectations: visualizations/ directory exists with full scaffolding
visualizations/intermediate_data/parsed_results.csv exists with 800 rows
visualizations/streamlit/index.py exists
Either streamlit/index.py or some file under streamlit/pages/ contains code that filters by 'site' and 'sensor' (uses st.selectbox/multiselect/radio for these columns)
Either streamlit/index.py or some file under streamlit/pages/ contains code that plots value over time (uses st.line_chart, sns.lineplot, or st.altair_chart with x=ts and y=value)
context.md was updated to mention the streamlit dashboard / filters / what page was added
interactive_page.sh exists and the agent did NOT actually run it (no live streamlit process needs to have been started)
